accessRequestWorkflowMapId.description: The id of the mapping entity that associates this secret to a specific access request workflow.

active.description: Whether the secret is in an active or deleted state.

autoChangeEnabled.description: Whether the secret’s password is automatically rotated on a schedule.

autoChangeNextPassword.description: Whether the secret should be flagged for immediate password change.

checkOutChangePasswordEnabled.description: Whether the secret’s password is automatically changed when a secret is checked in. This is a security feature that prevents a use of the password retrieved from check-out after the secret is checked in.

checkOutEnabled.description: Whether the user must check-out the secret to view it. Checking out gives the user exclusive access to the secret for a specified period or until the secret is checked in.

checkOutIntervalMinutes.description: The number of minutes that a secret will remain checked out.

comment.description: If the secret requires a comment to view or requires approval to view, a reason for accessing the secret must be provided.

doubleLockPassword.description: If the secret is DoubleLocked, this is the DoubleLock password needed to access the secret.

enableInheritPermissions.description: Whether the secret inherits permissions from the containing folder.

enableInheritSecretPolicy.description: Whether the secret policy is inherited from the containing folder.

folderId.description: If the secret is contained in a folder, the id of the containing folder. Set to null or -1 for secrets that are in the root folder.

forceCheckIn.description: Force the secret to be checked in, even if checked out by someone else. The user must have the "Force Check In" permission.

id.description: The id of the Secret to update. Must match the {id} in the path.

includeInactive.description: If the secret is deactivated, this must be set to true in order to access the secret. The user must also have the "View Inactive Secrets" permission.

items.description: A list of secret item field values.

launcherConnectAsSecretId.description: When an SSH secret is proxied, you can choose to connect as another user and then do an su to the current secret’s user. This is a common practice for connecting with a lower privileged account and then switching to the root user.

name.description: The name to display for the secret.

noAutoCheckout.description: Don't check out the secret automatically.

passwordTypeWebScriptId.description: The id of the password change script to use on applicable web password secrets.

proxyEnabled.description: Whether sessions launched on this secret use Secret Server’s proxying or connect directly.

requiresComment.description: Whether the user must enter a comment to view the secret.

secretPolicyId.description: The id of the secret policy that controls the security and other settings of the secret. Set to null to not assign a secret policy.

siteId.description: The id of the distributed engine site that is used by this secret for operations such as password changing.

ticketNumber.description: If the secret requires a comment to view or requires approval and a user must provide a help desk a ticket number, this is the ticket number to the help desk request.

ticketSystemId.description: If the secret requires a comment to view or requires approval and a user must provide a help desk a ticket number, this is the id of the help desk system configured in Secret Server that should be used to validate the ticket number.

webLauncherRequiresIncognitoMode.description: Whether the web launcher will require the browser to run in incognito mode.
